Perfect! I have all the file contents. Let me compile the report:

## Report: File Contents from /home/pgangavarapu/home/auto_docker_optimise

### base/Dockerfile
Complete Docker base image definition (283 lines). Key features:
- Based on ubuntu:24.04
- Sets up jovyan user (UID 1000) with conda environment
- Installs micromamba and Python 3.12
- Includes extensive system dependencies (build-essential, texlive, ffmpeg, etc.)
- Installs SciPy stack, R with bioconductor packages, and ab[x] immunology tools
- All user/permissions properly managed with fix-permissions script

### datascience/Dockerfile
Extends base image (143 lines). Adds:
- Java OpenJDK 17 for NGS tools
- Multiple bioinformatics tools: bcl2fastq, bases2fastq, FastQC, PANDAseq, sickle, CellRanger, dorado
- IgDiscover and cutadapt
- Updates PATH with all tool locations

### deeplearning/Dockerfile
Extends base image (62 lines). Adds:
- CUDA 12.4 toolkit and runtime
- Sets NVIDIA environment variables
- Installs AI/ML packages from requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt

### jupyterhub/datascience/Dockerfile
Extends datascience image (87 lines). Adds:
- JupyterHub and Jupyter components via conda
- JupyterLab extensions
- Mercury app with server extension disabled
- Health checks
- Start scripts and configuration files

### jupyterhub/deeplearning/Dockerfile
Extends deeplearning image (92 lines). Similar structure to datascience but:
- Adds jupyterlab-nvdashboard (nightly from rapidsai)
- Includes same JupyterLab/JupyterHub setup

### scripts/measure.py
Build and measure script (223 lines). Key functions:
- Builds Docker images with podman
- Records sizes to results.json
- Tracks build status and timing
- Supports building specific images or all
- Includes dependency resolution for parent images
- CLI with options: --label, --images, --no-build, --base-only

### scripts/optimize.py
Optimization ratchet loop (263 lines). Key features:
- Iteratively optimizes Dockerfiles using git for version control
- Keeps size-reducing changes, reverts non-improvements
- Tracks best total size across iterations
- Git commit/revert operations for changes
- Supports baseline measurement and status reporting
- CLI: --baseline, --status, --iterations, --images, --base-only, --files

### Requirements Files:

**requirements/scipy_mamba.txt** (38 packages)
Scientific Python stack: abnumber, anarci, altair, beautifulsoup4, black, bokeh, bottleneck, cloudpickle, conda-forge::blas, cython, dask, dill, h5py, ipympl, ipywidgets, matplotlib-base, modelcif, natsort, nodejs, numba, numexpr, openmm, openpyxl, pandas, patsy, pdbfixer, protobuf, pyarrow, pytables, scikit-image, scikit-learn, scipy, seaborn, sqlalchemy, statsmodels, sympy, widgetsnbextension, xlrd

**requirements/abx_pip.txt** (41 packages)
Immunology/bioinformatics: anndata, baltic, bbknn, biopython, celery, dask[complete], dnachisel, doubletdetection, duckdb, ete3, fastcluster, harmonypy, humanize, leidenalg, logomaker, matplotlib, mercury, mnemonic, paramiko, parasail, plottable, polars, prettytable, pyfamsa, pymongo, pypalettes (from git), pytest, python-circos, python-Levenshtein, pyyaml, s3cmd, sample-sheet, scanorama, scanpy, scrublet, scvelo, smart_open, tqdm, umap-learn, dm-tree, ml-collections

**requirements/r_mamba.txt** (26 packages)
R ecosystem: r-arrow, r-base, r-biocmanager, r-caret, r-crayon, r-devtools, r-dplyr, r-e1071, r-forecast, r-ggrepel, r-hexbin, r-htmltools, r-htmlwidgets, r-irkernel, r-nycflights13, r-pak, r-pheatmap, r-randomforest, r-rcurl, r-rmarkdown, r-rodbc, r-rsqlite, r-shiny, r-tidymodels, r-tidyverse, unixodbc

**requirements/r_cran.txt**
Single package: duckdb

**requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt** (8 packages)
Jupyter stack: jupyterhub-singleuser, jupyterlab, nbclassic, notebook>=7.2.2, jupyter-server-proxy

**requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt** (13 packages)
JupyterLab extensions & AI tools: jupyterlab-code-formatter, jupyterlab-github, jupyterlab-git, nbgitpuller, marimo>=0.19.11, marimo-jupyter-extension, jupyter_ai, ai21, anthropic, cohere, huggingface_hub, pillow, ipywidgets, openai, boto3

**requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt** (27 packages)
Deep learning & ML frameworks: torch, lightning, deepspeed, wandb, jax[cuda12_local], flax, chex, optax, equinox, keras, treescope, gradio, accelerate, datasets>=4.0.0, diffusers, evaluate, optimum, peft, transformers>=5.0.0

content.content:
1	# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
2	# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
3	# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.
4	
5	# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
6	# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
7	# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
8	# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license
9	
10	# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
11	# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble
12	
13	ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
14	FROM $BASE_IMG
15	
16	ARG OWNER=brineylab
17	LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'
18	
19	# NB user
20	ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
21	ARG NB_UID="1000"
22	ARG NB_GID="100"
23	
24	# Python version
25	ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12
26	
27	# Environmental variables
28	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
29	    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
30	    SHELL=/bin/bash \
31	    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
32	    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
33	    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
34	    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
35	    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
36	    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
37	ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
38	ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
39	ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"
40	
41	# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
42	# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
43	SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]
44	
45	
46	# ----------------------------
47	#       OS dependencies
48	# ----------------------------
49	USER root
50	RUN apt-get update --yes \
51	    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
52	    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
53	    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
54	    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
55	        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
56	        bzip2 \
57	        ca-certificates \
58	        locales \
59	        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
60	        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
61	        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
62	        netbase \
63	        sudo \
64	        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
65	        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
66	        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
67	        tini \
68	        wget \
69	        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
70	        bash \
71	        bashtop \
72	        btop \
73	        curl \
74	        htop \
75	        nano \
76	        nfs-common \
77	        openssh-server \
78	        pigz \
79	        screen \
80	        tmux \
81	        traceroute \
82	        unzip \
83	        vim \
84	        zip \
85	        gnupg \
86	        gnupg2 \
87	        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
88	        build-essential \
89	        # for latex labels
90	        cm-super \
91	        dvipng \
92	        # for matplotlib anim
93	        ffmpeg \
94	        fonts-liberation \
95	        git \
96	        # less is needed to run help in R
97	        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
98	        less \
99	        openssh-client \
100	        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
101	        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
102	        pandoc \
103	        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
104	        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
105	        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
106	        run-one \
107	        # nbconvert dependencies
108	        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
109	        texlive-xetex \
110	        texlive-fonts-recommended \
111	        texlive-plain-generic \
112	        tzdata \
113	        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
114	        xclip \
115	        # R pre-requisites
116	        fonts-dejavu \
117	        gfortran \
118	        gcc \
119	    && apt-get clean \
120	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
121	    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
122	    && locale-gen
123	
124	
125	# ----------------------------
126	#       User Setup
127	# ----------------------------
128	# Copy script to fix permissions
129	COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
130	RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
131	
132	# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
133	RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
134	    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
135	    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
136	    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc
137	
138	# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
139	# hadolint ignore=SC2046
140	RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
141	        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
142	    fi
143	
144	# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
145	# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
146	# Grant sudo and add password
147	RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
148	    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
149	    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
150	    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
151	    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
152	    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
153	    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
154	    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
155	    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
156	    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
157	    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER}
158	
159	# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
160	# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
161	# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
162	RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"
163	
164	
165	# ----------------------------
166	#         Micromamba
167	# ----------------------------
168	# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
169	#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
170	#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
171	#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
172	#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
173	USER ${NB_UID}
174	COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
175	WORKDIR /tmp
176	RUN set -x \
177	    && arch=$(uname -m) \
178	    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
179	        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
180	        arch="64"; \
181	    fi \
182	    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
183	    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
184	        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
185	    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
186	    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
187	    # Install the packages
188	    && ./bin/micromamba install \
189	        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
190	        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
191	        --yes \
192	        'jupyter_core' \
193	        'conda' \
194	        'mamba' \
195	        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
196	    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
197	    # Pin major.minor version of python
198	    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
199	    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
200	    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
201	    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
202	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
203	
204	
205	# ----------------------------
206	#         Directories
207	# ----------------------------
208	# Make requirements directory
209	# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility
210	RUN mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
211	    && mkdir "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
212	    && fix-permissions ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
213	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
214	
215	# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
216	# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
217	# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
218	RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"
219	
220	
221	#-----------------
222	#      SciPy
223	#-----------------
224	COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
225	RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
226	    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
227	    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
228	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
229	
230	
231	#-----------------------------
232	#   ab[x] and dependencies
233	#-----------------------------
234	COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
235	RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
236	
237	# ab[x]
238	# installed separately to prevent pip resolver issues
239	RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
240	        "scab>=0.2.4" \
241	        abstar \
242	        abutils
243	
244	
245	#-----------------
246	#       R
247	#-----------------
248	# R packages including IRKernel which gets installed globally.
249	COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
250	RUN mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
251	    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
252	    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
253	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
254	
255	# Install Bioconductor packages (BiocManager installed by mamba above)
256	RUN Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)"
257	
258	# install R packages that are not available in mamba with CRAN
259	# multi-thread compilation to speed up duckdb install - use 4 cores (25% of cores with xlarge runner)
260	COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
261	RUN MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
262	  pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
263	  install.packages( \
264	    pkgs, \
265	    dependencies = TRUE, \
266	    repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
267	  )"
268	
269	# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
270	# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
271	# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
272	RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"
273	
274	
275	# ----------------------------
276	#         Final Setup
277	# ----------------------------
278	USER ${NB_UID}
279	WORKDIR "${HOME}"
280	
281	ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
282	CMD ["/bin/bash"]
283

file.content:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER}

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Directories
# ----------------------------
# Make requirements directory
# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility
RUN mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && fix-permissions ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------------------
#   ab[x] and dependencies
#-----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt

# ab[x]
# installed separately to prevent pip resolver issues
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils


#-----------------
#       R
#-----------------
# R packages including IRKernel which gets installed globally.
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Install Bioconductor packages (BiocManager installed by mamba above)
RUN Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)"

# install R packages that are not available in mamba with CRAN
# multi-thread compilation to speed up duckdb install - use 4 cores (25% of cores with xlarge runner)
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
  pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
  install.packages( \
    pkgs, \
    dependencies = TRUE, \
    repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
  )"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.content:
1	ARG OWNER=brineylab
2	ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
3	FROM $BASE_IMG
4	
5	# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
6	# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
7	# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
8	LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'
9	
10	# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
11	# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
12	# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
13	# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license
14	
15	# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
16	ARG openjdk_version="17"
17	
18	
19	# ----------------------------
20	#       OS dependencies
21	# ----------------------------
22	USER root
23	RUN apt-get update --yes \
24	    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
25	        # for NGS tools
26	        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
27	        ca-certificates-java \
28	    && apt-get clean \
29	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
30	
31	
32	#-------------------
33	#     NGS TOOLS
34	#-------------------
35	# NGS directory
36	ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
37	RUN mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
38	    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
39	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"
40	WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"
41	
42	# bcl2fastq
43	RUN wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
44	    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
45	    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
46	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
47	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
48	
49	# bases2fastq
50	RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
51	    && tar xzvf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
52	    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
53	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
54	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
55	
56	# FastQC
57	RUN wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
58	    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
59	    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
60	    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
61	    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
62	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
63	
64	# PANDAseq
65	RUN apt-get update \
66	    && apt-get install -y \
67	        "libtool" \
68	        "automake" \
69	        "zlib1g-dev" \
70	        "libbz2-dev" \
71	        "pkg-config" \
72	    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
73	    && cd pandaseq \
74	    && ./autogen.sh \
75	    && ./configure \
76	    && make \
77	    && make install \
78	    && ldconfig
79	
80	# sickle
81	# requires zlib
82	RUN git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
83	    && cd zlib \
84	    && ./configure \
85	    && make \
86	    && make install \
87	    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
88	    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
89	    && cd sickle \
90	    && make \
91	    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
92	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
93	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
94	
95	# CellRanger
96	RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
97	    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
98	    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
99	    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
100	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
101	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
102	
103	# dorado (ONT)
104	RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
105	    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
106	    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
107	    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
108	    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
109	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
110	
111	# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
112	# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
113	# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
114	RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"
115	
116	# cutadapt + IgDiscover conda env must be installed as NB_UID (conda is owned by jovyan)
117	USER ${NB_UID}
118	RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
119	    "cutadapt" \
120	    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
121	    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
122	
123	# IgDiscover
124	RUN git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
125	    && cd igdiscover22 \
126	    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml
127	
128	RUN cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/igdiscover22" \
129	    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
130	    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
131	    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
132	    && mamba clean --all -f -y
133	
134	# update path with all tools
135	ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"
136	
137	
138	# ----------------------------
139	#         Final Setup
140	# ----------------------------
141	USER ${NB_UID}
142	WORKDIR "${HOME}"
143

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # for NGS tools
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


#-------------------
#     NGS TOOLS
#-------------------
# NGS directory
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
RUN mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"
WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# bcl2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# bases2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzvf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# FastQC
RUN wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# PANDAseq
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y \
        "libtool" \
        "automake" \
        "zlib1g-dev" \
        "libbz2-dev" \
        "pkg-config" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig

# sickle
# requires zlib
RUN git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# CellRanger
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# dorado (ONT)
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover conda env must be installed as NB_UID (conda is owned by jovyan)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
    "cutadapt" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# IgDiscover
RUN git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml

RUN cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/igdiscover22" \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

file.content:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# CUDA toolkit
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda~=12.4" \
        "cuda-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
        "cuda-command-line-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-compiler~=12.4" \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    # link cuda directory (installed by mamba) to /usr/local/cuda
    # without this, jax can't find libdevice.10.bc
    # see: https://github.com/jax-ml/jax/issues/4452
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/


# ----------------------------
#         AI/ML Packages
# ----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

65	HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
66	    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

71	RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

74	USER ${NB_UID}

81	USER ${NB_UID}
82	WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/datascience
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#       Mamba Packages
# ----------------------------
# installs JupyterLab, Jupyter Notebook, JupyterHub and NBClassic
# generates a Jupyter Server config
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    JupyterLab Extensions
# ----------------------------
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT

COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# mercury is installed as a Python package (for notebook widget use) 
# but its server extension breaks JupyterHub routing
RUN jupyter server extension disable mercury_app


# ----------------------------
#    Container Startup
# ----------------------------
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-notebook.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-singleuser.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/jupyter_server_config.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/docker_healthcheck.py /etc/jupyter/

# Fix permissions on /etc/jupyter as root
USER root
RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/

# HEALTHCHECK documentation: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck
# This healtcheck works well for `lab`, `notebook`, `nbclassic`, `server` and `retro` jupyter commands
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/915#issuecomment-1068528799
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

    && jupyter server --generate-config \

    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \

# https://github.com/rapidsai/jupyterlab-nvdashboard

    && mamba clean --all -f -y

RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/

# HEALTHCHECK documentation: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck
# This healtcheck works well for `lab`, `notebook`, `nbclassic`, `server` and `retro` jupyter commands
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/915#issuecomment-1068528799
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

Now I have full context. Let me start the optimization loop. 

**Optimization 1: Combine RUN layers in base/Dockerfile** - Merge the separate scipy, abx, R install steps into fewer layers and clean up in the same layer. Also remove the redundant `rm -rf .cache/` layers.

input.old_string:
#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------------------
#   ab[x] and dependencies
#-----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt

# ab[x]
# installed separately to prevent pip resolver issues
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils


#-----------------
#       R
#-----------------
# R packages including IRKernel which gets installed globally.
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Install Bioconductor packages (BiocManager installed by mamba above)
RUN Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)"

# install R packages that are not available in mamba with CRAN
# multi-thread compilation to speed up duckdb install - use 4 cores (25% of cores with xlarge runner)
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
  pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
  install.packages( \
    pkgs, \
    dependencies = TRUE, \
    repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
  )"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.

# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER}

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Directories
# ----------------------------
# Make requirements directory
# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility
RUN mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && fix-permissions ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------------------
#   ab[x] and dependencies
#-----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt

# ab[x]
# installed separately to prevent pip resolver issues
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils


#-----------------
#       R
#-----------------
# R packages including IRKernel which gets installed globally.
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Install Bioconductor packages (BiocManager installed by mamba above)
RUN Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)"

# install R packages that are not available in mamba with CRAN
# multi-thread compilation to speed up duckdb install - use 4 cores (25% of cores with xlarge runner)
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
  pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
  install.packages( \
    pkgs, \
    dependencies = TRUE, \
    repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
  )"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

# ----------------------------

# Make requirements directory
# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility

    && mkdir "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && fix-permissions ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER}

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Directories
# ----------------------------
# Make requirements directory
# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility
RUN mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && fix-permissions ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

lines: -# And setup work directory for backward-compatibility
-# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
-# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.

content.text:
Excellent! **Massive improvement: 115,257 MB → 86,728 MB (-28,529 MB, -24.7%)**. The layer combination saved nearly 5.7 GB from the base image alone, and that cascades to all child images. Keeping this commit.

Now let me do the same for the datascience Dockerfile - it has many separate RUN layers for each NGS tool.

**Optimization 2: Combine RUN layers in datascience/Dockerfile**

input.old_string:
# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # for NGS tools
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


#-------------------
#     NGS TOOLS
#-------------------
# NGS directory
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
RUN mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"
WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# bcl2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# bases2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzvf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# FastQC
RUN wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# PANDAseq
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y \
        "libtool" \
        "automake" \
        "zlib1g-dev" \
        "libbz2-dev" \
        "pkg-config" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig

# sickle
# requires zlib
RUN git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# CellRanger
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# dorado (ONT)
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover conda env must be installed as NB_UID (conda is owned by jovyan)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
    "cutadapt" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# IgDiscover
RUN git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml

RUN cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/igdiscover22" \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y

# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies + NGS TOOLS
# ----------------------------
USER root
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle ---
    && git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/zlib" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y

        ca-certificates-java \

    #--- PANDAseq ---

    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \

    #--- dorado ---
