toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.text:
**78,360 → 78,055 MB (-305 MB)**. Modest but clean win. Keeping it.

**Optimization 11: Use CUDA runtime instead of full toolkit** — The full `cuda-toolkit` includes compilers (nvcc), headers, and development libraries that are only needed for building CUDA code. If the AI/ML packages (PyTorch, JAX, etc.) bring their own CUDA runtime, we might only need the runtime.

Actually, this is risky - JAX with `jax[cuda12_local]` expects local CUDA. Let me try `cuda-runtime` + `cuda-nvcc` (needed for JIT compilation):

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
        "cuda-nvcc~=12.4" \
        "cuda-cudart-dev~=12.4" \
        "cuda-libraries-dev~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies + NGS TOOLS
# ----------------------------
USER root
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        libltdl-dev \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle (uses system zlib from zlib1g-dev) ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" \
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/.git" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/datascience
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#       Mamba + Extensions
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && jupyter server extension disable mercury_app \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    Container Startup
# ----------------------------
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-notebook.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-singleuser.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/jupyter_server_config.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/docker_healthcheck.py /etc/jupyter/

# Fix permissions on /etc/jupyter as root
USER root
RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/ \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

content.text: Interesting! The base image has `nvidia` (2.7 GB), `torch` (937 MB), and `triton` (290 MB) installed — these are leaking from pip dependency resolution. These should only be in the deeplearning image! The abx_pip packages must be pulling them in.

content.text: I'll install CPU-only torch before the abx packages so harmonypy finds it already satisfied:

input.old_string:
#--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
input.new_string:
#--- install CPU-only torch first (harmonypy depends on torch, avoid pulling CUDA libs) ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels (cm-super removed - uses Type1 fonts, very large)
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texlive/texmf-dist/doc \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texmf/doc \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

The `/opt/conda/lib/libcublas*` are symlinks pointing to `/opt/conda/targets/`. So the actual libraries live in `targets/`. We can't delete targets. But we can check for duplicate/unnecessary CUDA files.

Let me check if we can remove the include files and development headers:

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
        "cuda-nvcc~=12.4" \
        "cuda-cudart-dev~=12.4" \
        "cuda-libraries-dev~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
        "cuda-nvcc~=12.4" \
        "cuda-cudart-dev~=12.4" \
        "cuda-libraries-dev~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/stubs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/targets/x86_64-linux/include" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/include/cuda" "${CONDA_DIR}/include/cub"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels (cm-super removed - uses Type1 fonts, very large)
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texlive/texmf-dist/doc \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texmf/doc \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \

        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \

    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- install CPU-only torch first (harmonypy depends on torch, avoid pulling CUDA libs) ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:

LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels (cm-super removed - uses Type1 fonts, very large)
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \

        pandoc \

        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \

        xclip \

        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \

    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \

    && rm -rf /usr/share/texmf/doc \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group

    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \

    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- install CPU-only torch first (harmonypy depends on torch, avoid pulling CUDA libs) ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \

    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/locale" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/gconv" \

content.text: These are development headers used for building. They shouldn't be needed at runtime. But the deeplearning image installs CUDA and needs to symlink to `/usr/local/cuda` - removing include might break `jax`. Let me be more selective - just remove the sysroot locale and gconv, which are definitely safe:

#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- install CPU-only torch first (harmonypy depends on torch, avoid pulling CUDA libs) ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/locale" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/gconv" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/include" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.text:
Numba pulls in `cuda-nvrtc` and `libcufft` via conda. Since numba is in the scipy requirements, this is expected. But 393 MB of CUDA libs in the base image is significant. These get duplicated in every child image.

Since we can't remove numba (it's in requirements), let me see if we can prevent the CUDA deps from being pulled in. Numba doesn't actually need CUDA at runtime if GPU isn't used — it's optional. But conda resolves the full dependency tree.

Let me try a different approach — install numba with `--no-deps` and only its Python dependencies, avoiding the CUDA libs:

Actually, that would be fragile. Let me instead just remove these specific CUDA files in the cleanup step since they're not needed for CPU-only usage in base:

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels (cm-super removed - uses Type1 fonts, very large)
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texlive/texmf-dist/doc \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/texmf/doc \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- install CPU-only torch first (harmonypy depends on torch, avoid pulling CUDA libs) ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/locale" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/x86_64-conda-linux-gnu/sysroot/lib64/gconv" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.so' -exec strip --strip-unneeded {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# Remove build-time-only system packages after all compilation is done
USER root
RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove \
        build-essential \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
        g++ \
        cpp \
        make \
        dpkg-dev \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
